business.headline: RBI changes auto-debit rules: Your Netflix, SIPs and bill payments may get easierThe RBI has raised the no-OTP auto-debit limit to Rs 15,000, making recurring payments like subscriptions, SIPs, insurance premiums and bills smoother, faster and less prone to failure.

NSE nears IPO after Sebi panel clears Rs 1,800 crore settlementThe Securities and Exchange Board of India (Sebi) expert panel has approved NSE's proposal to settle long-pending cases by paying around Rs 1,800 crore.

Sensex tumbles 800 points: 4 things investors must knowAt around 9:50 am, the BSE Sensex was down 628.98 points, or 0.79%, at 78,644.35, while the NSE Nifty50 slipped 162.80 points, or 0.66%, to 24,413.80.

HCLTech, TCS, Infosys tumble up to 9%: Why are IT stocks falling today?At around 9:18 am, HCLTech was the biggest hit among IT stocks, plunging 9.1% to Rs 1,310.05 after its earnings update. Infosys fell 2.5% to Rs 1,280.85, Tata Consultancy Services slipped 1.7% to Rs 2,565, while Tech Mahindra dropped 3.8% to Rs 1,443.30.

HCLTech shares crash 10% after Q4 results. Why is the stock falling?The stock fell 9.64% to Rs 1,302.60 at 9:18 am, making it the biggest loser among frontline technology names. Other IT stocks also fell sharply in early trade.

Sensex, Nifty tank in early trade, dragged by IT stocks; HCLTech falls 10%The S&P BSE Sensex was down by 580.11 points to 78,693.22, while the NSE Nifty50 lost 142.60 points to 24,434.00 as of 9:42 am.

Why your favourite Diet Coke is suddenly disappearing from stores this summerSupply bottlenecks, delayed shipments and a summer demand surge are making Diet Coke harder to find on store shelves and quick-commerce apps across several cities.

RBI updates e-mandate rules for transactions over Rs 15,000. Check detailsUnder the new guidelines, recurring payments of up to Rs 15,000 will not require a one-time password (OTP). However, transactions above this threshold will need additional authentication.

India must pursue energy independence; fuel price cushion won't last: Gita GopinathIndia needs to speed up its shift towards energy independence as the current strategy of holding fuel prices steady becomes increasingly difficult to sustain, former IMF chief economist Gita Gopinath said, warning that the economy's present resilience could come under pressure if the Iran-US war prolongs.

Gita Gopinath decodes Iran war's impact on India and global economyIn an exclusive interview with India Today Consulting Editor Rajdeep Sardesai, Harvard University professor and former IMF Deputy Managing Director Gita Gopinath discussed the economic implications of the ongoing Iran conflict.
Sensex ends 753 points higher, Nifty climbs over 24,500; HUL up 4%The S&P BSE Sensex jumped 753.03 points to close at 79,273.33, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 211.75 points to end at 24,576.60.

Can you skip notice period after quitting your job? What the law really saysFor many employees eager to move on to their new job, one question keeps coming up: do you really have to serve those 30, 60 or 90 days, or can you leave sooner?

Groww shares hit 52-week high: What's driving the rally and should you buy now?Groww stock up 10%: The main trigger behind the rally is strong earnings performance. The company, operated by Billionbrains Garage Ventures, reported a 122% jump in net profit to Rs 686 crore in the March quarter.

As global crude oil crisis deepens, the only cure may be a demand shockReopening the Strait of Hormuz may gradually ease supply pressures, but the world may still need lower fuel demand to stop the crisis from spilling into prices, inflation and growth. Read on to know why demand destruction may be needed to restore balance between oil supply and demand.

Sensex jumps 700 points: Why is stock market rising today?The BSE Sensex surged over 500 points to trade at 79,022.78, up 502.48 points or 0.64% around 10 am. The Nifty 50 also gained momentum, rising 130.90 points or 0.54% to 24,495.75.

Tim Cook net worth: How rich is Apple's outgoing CEO after 15-year run?Tim Cook joined Apple in 1998, after working at IBM for 12 years and a brief stint at Compaq. At Apple, he initially handled operations and supply chain, an area where he built a strong reputation.
Sensex, Nifty trade higher over hopes of peace talks between Iran and USThe S&P BSE Sensex added 370.07 points to 78,890.37, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 100.05 points to 24,464.90 as of 9:42 am.

Apple shares rise 1% after Tim Cook exit, John Ternus named CEOThe stock closed at $273.05, up $2.82 or 1.04%, reflecting a measured but positive reaction from investors to the unexpected leadership change as John Ternus steps up to lead Apple.

Who is John Ternus, the man set to replace Tim Cook at AppleTernus will take over from September 1, while Cook will move into the role of executive chairman, marking a shift in leadership at one of the world's most valuable companies.

Why did DA increase by only 2% this time? Here's the real reasonThe latest DA hike has come in at just 2%, leaving many employees surprised. With expectations going up to 4%, why did the increase stay at the lower end?

entertainment.headline: Are Harry Styles, Zoe Kravitz engaged? Actor's diamond ring adds fuel to rumours Harry Styles and Zoe Kravitz have sparked engagement rumours after being seen together in London, with Kravitz seen wearing a striking diamond ring.

K-pop group CORTIS sharpens focus with REDRED ahead of GREENGREEN K-pop group CORTIS, under BigHit Music, revealed REDRED as a pre-release track for their second project, GREENGREEN. The track highlights their evolving sound and hands-on involvement in production.

I wasn't jailed because of money: Rajpal Yadav opens up on Rs 9 crore case Actor Rajpal Yadav clarified that his jail term was not due to financial inability but a matter of principle in a Rs 9 crore case, highlighting complexities beyond money.

Bhooth Bangla box office Day 5: Exceptional weekday run after Rs 100-crore haul Akshay Kumar's Bhooth Bangla has made a strong box office debut, grossing over Rs 117 crore worldwide. The film marks a successful reunion with director Priyadarshan and features the late Asrani's final screen appearance.

Kim Kardashian, Lewis Hamilton seen kissing during romantic Malibu getaway Kim Kardashian and Lewis Hamilton were seen sharing a kiss during a Malibu beach outing, with several photos and videos going viral.

Zeenat Aman is not a good actor: Mumtaz asks 'how many awards did she get?' Veteran actor Mumtaz recently commented that Zeenat Aman is not a very good actor and questioned the number of awards she received.

Toaster director shares how Rajkummar Rao, Patralekhaa brought Farah Khan on board Farah Khan is receiving praise for her cameo in Toaster, with director Vivek Das Chaudhary crediting Rajkummar Rao and Patralekhaa for bringing her on board. The filmmaker also expressed hope to see more of Farah on screen.

Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 34: Ranveer's film sees slight incline on 5th Tuesday Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge has set new benchmarks in Indian cinema with its outstanding box office performance. After a dip in numbers on Monday, the Aditya Dhar film saw a slight incline in collection on its 5th Tuesday.

Chiyaan Vikram has 4 films coming. But is he addressing the right problem? Chiyaan Vikram, once a trailblazer in Tamil cinema, faced box office challenges post-pandemic. He is set for a fresh start with four new films. Industry and fans await the return of his iconic versatility and bold performances.

Shah Rukh Khan eyes Jawan 2 after King: Sources Shah Rukh Khan is set to return with Jawan 2, with the script reportedly locked and plans underway to begin work after completing King. The sequel to the 2023 blockbuster is expected to go on floors next year.

Matka King, Dahaad, Darlings, Mirzapur: Vijay Varma aces being evil next door Vijay Varma is reorganising the way evil is shown on Hindi screen by bringing alive unsettling traits in everyday men. His roles in Darlings, Dahaad and Matka King show the scariest kind of evil is often hidden in those that we deem normal.

Ben Affleck, Matt Damon to receive Robin Williams Legacy of Laughter Award Ben Affleck and Matt Damon will receive the Robin Williams Legacy of Laughter Award, honouring their bond with the late Robin Williams. The award is to be presented by Williams' children at a San Francisco event.

New Dhurandhar film in theatres? Aditya Dhar likely to release BTS documentary After the massive success of Dhurandhar and its sequel, Aditya Dhar is reportedly planning a first-of-its-kind theatrical release of a behind-the-scenes (BTS) film based on the franchise. The project will feature exclusive BTS footage and first interviews of stars like Ranveer Singh and Arjun Rampal.

Father-to-be Ranveer beams as paps congratulate him on Deepika's pregnancy Ranveer Singh is beaming with joy as he receives warm wishes from fans following Deepika Padukone's second pregnancy announcement. The couple shared the joyous news on April 19.

Citadel 2 trailer: Priyanka Chopra, Richard Madden face new threats, betrayals The trailer for Citadel Season 2 has been unveiled, promising thrilling action and intricate global conspiracies with Priyanka Chopra Jonas and Richard Madden returning to lead.

Anne Hathaway says Inshallah during interview. Watch video here Anne Hathaway recently spoke about ageing and life in an interview, where her use of the phrase "Inshallah" caught attention online. She will be seen next in The Devil Wears Prada 2.

Sydney Sweeney's cameo cut from The Devil Wears Prada 2. Here's why Sydney Sweeney's cameo in The Devil Wears Prada 2 has been cut from the final film, despite being shot. The sequel, starring Anne Hathaway and Meryl Streep, is set to release in theatres on May 1.

Director Abhishek Shah on National Award pressure: Every film has its own fate Abhishek Shah, National Award-winning director of Hellaro, is all set to release his new film, Dhabkaaro. In an interview with India Today, Shah said he prioritises reaching wider audiences over chasing box office numbers.

Deepika Padukone back on set post pregnancy news, director Punit Malhotra shares pic Looks like Deepika Padukone has resumed work days after announcing her second pregnancy with Ranveer Singh. The actor was recently seen on set for a maternity campaign, with director Punit Malhotra.

general news.headline: Trump credits Pak's Asim Munir, cites fractured Iran as he extends ceasefireUS President Donald Trump extended the Iran ceasefire after a request from Pakistan, citing appeals by Asim Munir and Shehbaz Sharif, while maintaining the Hormuz blockade and military readiness until a unified proposal is submitted.

Just weeks into office, Nepal PM Balen Shah faces protests over student union banThe unrest has been triggered by the scrapping of student unions, a controversial customs duty on goods from India, and growing demands for Home Minister Sudan Gurung's resignation over alleged financial irregularities.

Get out, you are causing jam: Frustrated woman berates BJP minister at rallyFrustrated by the standstill, a Mumbai woman stepped out of her vehicle, walked into the middle of the march, confronted the BJP minister and got into a heated argument with police officers.

How air defence unit in Sirsa foiled Pak ballistic missile threat during Op SindoorA Pakistani ballistic missile, believed to be from the Fateh or Shaheen series, was intercepted mid-air over Haryana during May last year before it could reach Delhi. The neutralisation was carried out by an IAF unit stationed at Sirsa, led by Air Commodore Rohit Kapil.

Why your favourite Diet Coke is suddenly disappearing from stores this summerSupply bottlenecks, delayed shipments and a summer demand surge are making Diet Coke harder to find on store shelves and quick-commerce apps across several cities.

How Gulf oil giants are racing to bypass Hormuz after Iran war shockIran's wartime shutdown of the Strait of Hormuz has jolted Gulf economies, triggering a costly scramble to build pipelines and revive trade corridors, as oil producers seek to reduce dependence on a vulnerable maritime choke point.

US tourist raped, confined to room at Karnataka homestay; owner, staff arrestedAn American tourist was allegedly raped by a homestay worker in Karnataka's Kodagu, with the owner accused of confining and threatening her.

After Virat Kohli cut ties, Bengaluru's One8 Commune outlet shuts over rent duesVirat Kohli had already severed ties with the outlet and withdrawn his brand name from the restaurant after repeated compliance issues.

Gen Z cracked the 'do more, get more work' cycle - Mumbai man's post goes viralA man from Mumbai explained why Gen Z employees avoid going "above and beyond," emphasising how high performers often faced burnout instead of rewards.

Live: Iran forces fire at container ship, UK agency claims major damageThe fragile ceasefire in the US-Israel war on Iran has been extended, offering a narrow window for diplomacy even as uncertainty persists over whether talks can prevent a return to conflict. US President Donald Trump has indicated he remains prepared to resume military action if negotiations fail, while simultaneously calling for a unified proposal from Tehran. Efforts to restart dialogue, however, remain uneven. Iran has yet to confirm participation in proposed talks in Pakistan, and US Vice President JD Vance's planned visit to Islamabad has now been indefinitely cancelled. Tensions continue to simmer around the Strait of Hormuz, where the US naval blockade remains a key flashpoint, viewed by Tehran as a breach of ceasefire terms. Beyond the diplomatic impasse, the wider region remains volatile. Israeli operations have continued in Lebanon despite a ceasefire, with Beirut calling for internal unity ahead of fresh engagements with Israel in Washington. As the extended deadline unfolds, the risk of escalation remains high if diplomatic momentum fails to translate into a concrete agreement. Catch all the live updates at indiatoday.in.

South vs North: When Nari Shakti politics re-opened a dangerous trust deficitWhat began as a debate over women's representation in Parliament last week quickly reopened a far more contentious question: delimitation, and with it, the spectre of a North-South divide.

India will not bow to any form of terror: PM Modi on Pahalgam attack anniversaryPM Modi, in a post on X, paid tribute to the victims of the Pahalgam terror attack, in which terrorists killed 26 people, mostly tourists, saying they "will never be forgotten."

Aditya Dhar likely to release BTS documentaryAfter the massive success of Dhurandhar and its sequel, Aditya Dhar is reportedly planning a first-of-its-kind theatrical release of a behind-the-scenes (BTS) film based on the franchise. The project will feature exclusive BTS footage and first interviews of stars like Ranveer Singh and Arjun Rampal.

UP bureaucrat enters Uttarakhand temple sanctum with armed guard, priests objectThe presence of a firearm within the innermost shrine sparked immediate objections from priests, who termed it a violation of the temple's religious sanctity. The incident has led to an enquiry, even as authorities put up signage restricting weapons inside the temple complex.

RBI changes auto-debit rules: Your Netflix, SIPs and bill payments may get easierThe RBI has raised the no-OTP auto-debit limit to Rs 15,000, making recurring payments like subscriptions, SIPs, insurance premiums and bills smoother, faster and less prone to failure.

TCS Nashik survivor recalls horror, says colleague groped her, abused her godsThe survivor alleged a "scary" workplace where accused employees acted with impunity under senior officials' protection. She cited an instance where one accused made derogatory religious remarks, questioning how many wives a god had and how a god could not know about his own child.

A jamdani for Bengal: Kangana Ranaut's quiet strategic style moveKangana Ranaut who was in West Bengal campaigning for the 2026 elections, participated in a roadshow in Nandigram and what she wore was both rooted and deliberate.

iPhone is now more affordable in India, full credit goes to Apple CEO Tim CookThe iPhone has become more affordable in India. Yes, hear us out. Tim Cook, who is going to be replaced by John Ternus as CEO from September 1, has indeed made it easier for Apple users in this country.

Yellow alert in Delhi, heatwaves to hit these states from todayHeatwave conditions are sweeping across large parts of India, with the IMD warning that temperatures will continue to rise in the days ahead. Is your state on the list?

AI Zuckerberg to PC tracking: Meta ramps AI push ahead of layoffsMeta is implementing new measures to track employee mouse movements and keystrokes to train AI. This move comes at a time when the Mark Zuckerberg-led firm is rumoured to be planning to layoff as many as 15,000 employees. Here are all the details.

MBA graduate ditches Rs 25 LPA job to become taxi driver, earns Rs 6.5 lakh monthlyAn MBA graduate from a Tier-2 college left behind a Rs 25 lakh job offer to become a taxi driver. Three years later, his journey took an unexpected turn.

Shrinking, drying, fading: Bihar's beloved litchi is changing foreverClimate on My Plate is India Today Science's series on how the climate crisis is reshaping the everyday things you eat, drink, buy and own. This week, it is the litchi you reach for every summer, but the beloved fruit is being devoured by the climate crisis.

Bengaluru woman ties up lover to 'propose in western style', burns him aliveAccording to police, Prema told Kiran she would propose to him in a "western style," where one partner gets down on one knee and expresses their love. She then tied his hands and legs with a rope and blindfolded him.

Rs 25 lakh for coaching, 99 percentile in JEE Mains, yet no IITEvery year, lakhs of students chase the IIT dream, armed with years of preparation and immense family sacrifice. Now, more than a lakh score above 90 percentile, and thousands score 99. Yet for most, even that still isn't enough.

sports.headline: Premier League glory in 2016, Leicester City relegated to England's 3rd division Leicester City have been relegated to League One after a draw against Hull City. The 2016 Premier League champions complete a stunning fall, dropping to England's third tier after back-to-back relegations and a troubled campaign.

Next demi-god of cricket? BCCI urged to act fast on Vaibhav Sooryavanshi Kris Srikkanth urged the BCCI to fast-track 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi into the Indian senior team, suggesting that he could be the next legendary figure in the sport after Sachin Tendulkar.

SRH vs DC: Delhi Capitals reflect on Nitish Rana ploy that ultimately backfired Delhi Capitals' Venugopal Rao addresses the controversial decision to bowl Nitish Rana for his full quota and the tactical middle-order collapse that handed SRH a 47-run victory in Hyderabad.

World body revokes India Volleyball Federation recognition after players' protest FIVB has revoked recognition of Volleyball Federation of India after governance breaches. The move comes days after player protests exposed deeper issues around facilities, selection politics and athlete welfare in Indian volleyball.

FIFA adds more World Cup 2026 tickets after backlash over pricey new categories FIFA faces backlash after hiking FIFA World Cup 2026 ticket prices and adding premium categories. The fresh ticket release aims to ease concerns, but questions over transparency and affordability remain unresolved.

What makes Sakib Hussain stand out? Aaron hails SRH pacer after crucial DC spell IPL 2026: Varun Aaron pinpoints the key trait behind Sakib Hussain's rise after SRH's win over DC. His pace variation and hunger are turning Hyderabad's bowling from a weakness into a genuine strength.

LSG vs RR: Sooryavanshi in town as Lucknow hope to escape their backyard trap IPL 2026, RR vs LSG preview: Lucknow Super Giants are trapped in their own labyrinth. With their big guns misfiring and the season sliding away, the task only gets tougher. Now, a determined Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and the Rajasthan Royals have arrived in town, looking to find their own path back to winning ways on Wednesday.

Abhishek Sharma matches Virat Kohli, eclipses Chris Gayle with blazing 135: In Stats IPL 2026, SRH vs DC: Abhishek Sharma was in outstanding form on Tuesday, scoring an unbeaten 135 off 68 balls for Sunrisers Hyderabad against Delhi Capitals, guiding the Orange Army to a 47-run victory.

Play of the Day: Abhishek Sharma bluffs DC into bowling 4 overs off Nitish Rana SRH vs DC: Abhishek Sharma punished Delhi Capitals for the series of errors that they committed at the Uppal on Tuesday. Some smart batting from the young left-hander forced Delhi into committing a cardinal sin: bowling their part-timer for 4 full overs.

Rahul's batting intent, wicketkeeping skills questioned after DC stumble vs SRH Aakash Chopra criticised KL Rahul's batting intent and wicketkeeping after Delhi Capitals' heavy defeat to Sunrisers Hyderabad in IPL 2026, questioning his approach in the 243-run chase. Rahul struggled with the bat and behind the stumps in the loss.

An off-day: Axar Patel downplays Delhi Capitals' heavy loss to Sunrisers Hyderabad IPL 2026: Delhi Capitals captain Axar Patel termed the 47-run loss to Sunrisers Hyderabad as an "off day," while admitting that fielding lapses cost his side despite Abhishek Sharma's explosive unbeaten 135 powering SRH to 242.

Put a camera on him: Abhishek Sharma reveals father's match ritual after 135 vs DCIPL 2026, SRH vs DC: Abhishek Sharma revealed a touching father-son ritual after his unbeaten 135 against Delhi Capitals where he won the Player of the Match award after the Sunrisers Hyderabad won by 47 runs.

Abhishek's Hyderabadi special gives DC bitter aftertaste of tactical errorsAbhishek Sharma's unbeaten 135 powered SRH to victory over Delhi Capitals as DC were left regretting poor tactical calls and a slow start in the chase in IPL 2026.

India Women unfazed by Deepti Sharma form slump ahead of T20 World CupSAW vs INDW, 3rd T20I: India Women remain confident in Deepti Sharma despite her prolonged slump, with coach Aavishkar Salvi backing her to bounce back soon. Deepti has scored only two runs and gone wicketless in the series against South Africa.

Abhishek unveils new celebration after hundred vs DC, reveals story behind itAbhishek Sharma revealed the reason behind his new celebration after scoring a stunning unbeaten 135 for Sunrisers Hyderabad against Delhi Capitals in IPL 2026.

An un-Abhishek hundred: Opener ditches all-out style, hits 2nd IPL hundredSRH vs DC IPL 2026: SunRisers Hyderabad opener Abhishek Sharma scored a terrific hundred in his team's home game against Delhi Capitals. Batting first, a patient Abhishek brought up his 2nd IPL century in 47 balls.

Narwal bags gold at Junior World Cup; India takes three medals on opening dayShiva Narwal won gold in the men's 10m air pistol as India secured three medals on the opening day of the ISSF Junior World Cup in Cairo. Panaah Bhugra claimed silver in the women's 10m air rifle, while Chirag Sharma added bronze to cap a strong start for India.

Dhoni sparks CSK return hopes before MI tie, does wicketkeeping drills in netsMS Dhoni has sparked hope for Chennai Super Kings fans ahead of the MI clash after returning to wicketkeeping drills in the nets at Wankhede during IPL 2026. There is no clrity from Chennai about Dhoni's return to the team.

Mumbai Indians get huge Will Jacks boost before blockbuster CSK clashIPL 2026: Mumbai Indians have been boosted by the return of Will Jacks ahead of their clash against Chennai Super Kings, following his break after a packed international schedule.

Fit and firing Mayank Yadav is causing selection headaches for LSG: MarkramIPL 2026: Lucknow Super Giants opener Aiden Markram has confirmed that pace sensation Mayank Yadav is 'fit and firing,' providing a potential 'X-factor' boost for a franchise. LSG are currently struggling with three consecutive losses.

How Tendulkar's viral tweet vindicates Jurel's relentless wicketkeeping workDhruv Jurel said Sachin Tendulkar's tweet felt like a dream come true after his stunning stumping of Cameron Green in RR's IPL 2026 clash against KKR. Jurel has been made the No.1 choice wicketkeeper by RR this season.

Australian teenager Gout Gout breaks Usain Bolt's U20 record. Legend reactsAustralian teenager Gout Gout shattered legendary Usain Bolt's youth 200m record. The Jamaican reacted to the feat and hoped that the teenager would surround himself with the correct type of people.

If Shreyas Iyer's has issues with not being selected for India, it's valid: ManjrekarIPL 2026: Former Indian cricketer Sanjay Manjrekar has labelled Shreyas Iyer's continued exclusion from the national T20 squad as 'unfair,' arguing that the captain's 'raw deal' from selectors is unjustified given his elite form. Iyer has since silenced critics by leading the Punjab Kings to the top of the 2026 IPL table while maintaining a staggering 182.46 strike rate.

Loan in RCB star: R Ashwin's suggestion to CSK after Ayush Mhatre injuryR Ashwin has suggested that Chennai Super Kings use the IPL loan system to bring in RCB's U-19 star Vihaan Malhotra after Ayush Mhatre was ruled out of IPL 2026 with injury.

Ashok Sharma can go places, one to watch out for: Jason Holder impressed with GT pacerJason Holder has backed Ashok Sharma as a bowler to watch, praising his rare natural pace and evolving skill set in the Indian Premier League 2026. He believes that with fitness, hunger, and experience, the young Gujarat Titans pacer can develop into a complete and dangerous fast bowler.

India cap can't be freely distributed: Ashwin not a fan of second-string teamsR Ashwin opposes second-string India T20I teams, stressing the value of the India cap amid a packed schedule, even as selectors consider giving young players chances in upcoming tours.

SRH vs DC Highlights: Abhishek, Malinga star as Hyderabad beat Delhi by 47 runsSRH vs DC Highlights, IPL 2026: Sunrisers Hyderabad beat the Delhi Capitals by 47 runs in Hyderabad and moved to third in the points table. Abhishek Sharma scored an unbeaten 135 after which Eshan Mlainga took four wickets to restrict the visiting team.

Mandhana falls down in ICC Women's T20I rankings after SA series flop showSmriti Mandhana dropped to fourth in the latest ICC Women's T20I rankings after a poor run against South Africa, while Shafali Verma climbed to sixth following her half-century in Durban.

Pakistan Cricket Board in talks to build a stadium in Saudi ArabiaPCB explores plans to build a stadium in Jeddah with Saudi cricket body, aiming to expand the game in the kingdom and potentially host PSL matches amid growing interest in cricket.

IPL 2026: CSK's Ayush Mhatre ruled out of the season due to hamstring injuryIPL 2026: Chennai Super Kings suffer a major setback as Ayush Mhatre is ruled out of IPL 2026 with injury. The youngster's absence leaves CSK searching for answers in an already struggling campaign.

Bishnoi gang linked to Canada cricket match-fixing scandal at T20 World CupLawrence Bishnoi gang's alleged links to Cricket Canada have triggered a major scandal. Claims of threats, intimidation and possible match-fixing during the ICC Men's T20 World Cup are now under investigation.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
